title: acalypha subcastrata
intro: Acalypha subcastrata, also known as Indian nettle or tiger's tail, is a tropical plant species belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Acalypha subcastrata include the treatment of skin conditions, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for snake bites and scorpion stings. Herbal preparations of Acalypha subcastrata include decoctions, infusions, and topical applications, which can be used to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
